items.title: Apology for 'appalling' display of autopsy body parts without consent

children.content: A formal apology is given in Tasmania's parliament for the past practice of taking human specimens from autopsies and displaying them in a museum without the knowledge of family members.

gallery.altText: Slices of body parts covered in Perspex and put on display on walls.
Two men stand outside parliament house, one holding a box that says 'They stole my brother's brain'.

items.title: The dead fish waste disaster that Tasmania failed to plan for

children.content: High-volume salmon mortalities last year swamped Tasmania's waste streams as companies scrambled to find somewhere to accept them, documents show. There are now concerns the state remains unprepared for future mass deaths.

items.title: Tribunal rules in favour of landlord in first test of new pet rental rules

children.content: A landlord's decision to deny a tenant's request to keep a 6-month-old rescue kitten in a unit has been upheld, despite new Tasmanian laws intended to make it easier to keep pets in rentals.

image.altText: A British Shorthair kitten.

items.title: It's time for Eric Abetz to tackle Tasmania's debt. How far will he go?

children.content: The lead-up to state budget day is normally a time for flashy funding announcements. But as Tasmania stares down the barrel of $7 billion of debt, things are looking a bit bleaker.

items.title: 'Silence is not consent': Mother raped by massage therapist

items.title: Death penalty rates at 40-year high globally, Amnesty International says

children.content: A crackdown in Iran drove a surge in the number of people executed worldwide in 2025, but China remains the "leading executioner," human rights group Amnesty International says.

items.title: WHO 'deeply concerned' over Ebola outbreak after at least 131 people die

items.title: Will the war in Iran make Australia use less plastic?

children.content: Could the war in Iran change the way we recycle back home?
Australia mostly relies on importing plastic from overseas.
As well as petrol and diesel, the Strait of Hormuz is a key delivery route for a crucial backbone of plastic production. But, that's now cut off.
It's led to a growing conversation about how Australia might transition to fully recycling plastic packaging onshore.

image.altText: TN_PlasticIran

items.title: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak 'for decades' spreads to WA, Qld and SA

children.content: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

image.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

items.title: Snowtown killer's suppression order lifts after 25 years

children.content: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image can be revealed after South Australia's highest court lifted a suppression order made 25 years ago.

items.title: Refunds but no price caps for ripped-off older Australians

children.content: Support at Home program price caps, due to start in July, have been deferred indefinitely. Hundreds of thousands of older people rely on the federal government program, which provides help with cleaning, showering and clinical supports like nursing.

image.altText: An elderly man sits in a rocking chair facing away into a sunlit garden, next to a vase of flowers on a table.

items.title: NT records two deaths from rare mosquito-borne disease

children.content: Two people have died from Murray Valley encephalitis in Alice Springs, sparking a health alert for the rare mosquito-borne disease across the Northern Territory.

image.altText: A female Aedes aegypti mosquito bites someone's skin.

items.title: Stranded driver, students among 10 rescues during heavy rainfall in NSW and Qld

children.content: Almost 50 high school students and teachers have been rescued after becoming stranded by floodwater at a national park in the Scenic Rim while on a school camp.
Earlier, near the New South Wales and Queensland border, crews worked to rescue a person stuck in a vehicle at Numinbah Valley near Murwillumbah.
NSW SES volunteers and emergency service partners have responded to 197 incidents since Monday afternoon.
